author: @joeglory425
text: This looks promising for putting a finely dispersed zinc oxide layer on expanded graphite.  Very interesting.  Cheers!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: glad you found it useful mate

replies.author: @joeglory425
replies.text: Definitely. There's a wealth of information on your channel. I watched this video when it first came out, thought it neat, but it wasn't relevant to anything I was doing at the time. What brought me back to this video was I was looking for a way to disperse zinc oxide into graphene/expanded graphite and the smaller the particles of zinc oxide the better (at least my intuition says so). I had actually hit on the idea of using zinc chloride intercalated graphite and then expanding it in the microwave as a way of making nanoparticulate zinc oxide dispersed in expanded graphite so I searched your channel to see if you'd done that. This was one of the videos that came up. I've since discarded the idea of using zinc chloride intercalated graphite in favor of going the zinc acetate route. All of this has given me a new idea to work on separate from my current project. It all grows from a tiny seed, doesn't it? For me the seed was carbon nanotubes made in a microwave and the audacity that cutting edge science could be done by anyone at home using commonly available materials. So good job, Rob! Cheers.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's smart thinking mate and your right a little audacious lol - very cool

author: @matta8508

Cool! I was trying to react the zinc directly with heated acetic acid cause I was to lazy to actually go through making zinc carbonate... But it really proved to be a bad idea since the reaction is so painfully slow that it makes me feel like going out and watch the grass grow.
Meanwhile I noticed that I actually had all the reagents to make the Zn-Acetate as I was supposed to, with even some reagent grade stuff LOL

replies.author: @dizziedice9568
replies.text:
+Robert Murray-Smith Nice! I was going to ask you this question, but it was a bit scared you couldn't answer since it goes 2 years since the publication of this video.

My interest in this is to create film-based solar cells, and some oled, perhaps in the future a small touchscreen for investigation purposes... There's much more into the process as some lab equipment is missing (magnetic stirrer/heater) etc, that's what scares me the most.


I have a question, did you do "eyeball" the quantities or have you measurd the molar masses?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: measured

replies.author: @dizziedice9568
replies.text:
+Robert Murray-Smith Thank you, I just favourited this video. I'm still trying to understand the sol-gel process, I saw a paper which required Zn nitrates and Al nitrates to form the ZnO:Al .



I'm very interesting on doing this actually..! I had to review all the Chemistry I've learnt about 12 years ago, hard task, especially when alone but... acheived! :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - good on ya mate - and good luck with it

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Thank you. I usually have a specific project in mind and this one is for a precursor for making aluminium doped zinc oxide for thin transparent condutive film for solar cells - I am in Sri Lanka at the moment working on graphite but i will get back to this on my return to the UK
